title: Fumana juniperina
intro: Fumana juniperina, also known as Juniperum fruticans, is a perennial shrub belonging to the family Cistaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fumana juniperina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Fumana juniperina is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Fumana juniperina is classified within the family Cistaceae, commonly known as the rockrose family. This small shrub is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of North Africa. Taxonomically, Fumana juniperina is classified in the genus Fumana, which includes several other species of rockrose plants. The family Cistaceae is part of the larger clade of asterids in the angiosperm classification. The plant's classification highlights its unique characteristics and adaptations to its Mediterranean habitat.
uses: Fumana juniperina, also known as Rock rose or Juniper Fumana, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Its leaves and flowers are used to create infusions and decoctions for various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for wound healing and skin conditions. It is also used to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. benefits: Fumana juniperina, also known as dwarf fumana, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant is thought to possess antioxidant properties, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. constituents: Fumana juniperina contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to the plant's potential medicinal value. The flavonoids present in Fumana juniperina, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antitumor activities. The terpenoids, including limonene and pinene, may contribute to the plant's antiseptic and antiviral properties.

preparations: Fumana juniperina is a Mediterranean plant species that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine.
